seoTitle: How to Add a Clickable Link to a Canva Video
metaDescription: Learn how to add a clickable link to your Canva video so viewers can jump straight to your website, product, or booking page.
intro: Turning part of a video into a clickable link lets you drive viewers directly to a website, product page, or booking form, which is valuable for marketing and promotional content. This guide covers how to add that link inside Canva.

steps.heading: Select the element to link
steps.body: Choose a text overlay, button graphic, or shape within your video that you want viewers to click.

steps.heading: Open the link option
steps.body: Click the link icon in the toolbar once the element is selected.

steps.heading: Enter your destination URL
steps.body: Paste in the website, product page, or booking link you want the click to lead to.

steps.heading: Confirm the link is active for its full display time
steps.body: Make sure the linked element stays visible on screen long enough for viewers to click it.

steps.heading: Share or export your video
steps.body: Share your video as a Canva link to keep the click functionality, since standard exported video files won't carry the link.

faq.question: Will the clickable link work if I download the video as an MP4?
faq.answer: No, links only work when the video is viewed through a Canva share link or embedded presentation, not in a downloaded video file.

faq.question: Can I link different elements to different URLs in the same video?
faq.answer: Yes, each element can have its own separate link destination.

faq.question: Is this feature useful for social media videos?
faq.answer: It's best suited for videos shared via a Canva link, website embed, or presentation rather than uploaded directly to platforms like Instagram.

faq.question: Do I need Canva Pro to add clickable links?
faq.answer: No, adding links to elements is available on the free plan.
